settings.title: Drop In to Our Summer Coat Clinic in May
settings.excerpt: Before the hot months arrive, bring your dog by for free advice on whether that thick coat helps or hurts.
settings.body:
Every summer we hear the same plan: shave the dog right down to keep it cool. For many breeds that is exactly the wrong move.

It seems logical, less fur for less heat, but a dog's coat does not work the way a jumper does. For a lot of breeds, taking it off makes summer harder, not easier.

Why shaving can backfire

A double coat actually insulates against heat and protects pale skin from sunburn. Shaving it can leave a dog hotter and at risk, not cooler. In some breeds the coat never grows back quite the same, either.

What usually helps a dog far more in hot weather:

  • A thorough de-shed to clear the dead undercoat that traps heat.
  • Regular brushing at home to keep air moving through the coat.
  • Shade and water, which matter more than coat length anyway.

Drop in this May

So through May we are running a free coat clinic. Drop in with your dog and we will tell you honestly what its particular coat needs for summer, whether that is a tidy, a thorough de-shed, or simply more brushing at home.

No appointment needed for the advice, and there is no obligation to book anything afterwards. We will be glad to meet your dog either way, and to settle the shave-or-not question for your particular breed.
